And if they had time to prepare boiling pitch or oil, they knew the attack was coming. Interesting.

comment_text:
Already, this invasion scene reminds us that Mizu is, ironically, _not_ a samurai. She holds no titles, serves no Lord, and lays claim to no righteous path.

Mizu is not a samurai, or a ronin, or a hero. She is a killer. A demon consumed by revenge. She will do whatever it takes to claim her vengeance, even if it means using others as meat shields and tools.

It's tragic, but oh so interesting.

comment_text: Fantastic series so far. Immensely enjoyable. My only nit pick, which is a bit of a sticker for me, is the fact she’s blue eyed. I know I know, this is a fiction but  Brown eyes are dominant over blue. If you have one brown eyed parent, unless they carry the recessive blue gene, and a blue eyed parent, all the offspring will be brown eyed. To be blue eyed you must have a copy of the blue gene from each parent. If you have a copy of the brown and a copy of the blue you’ll still be brown eyed. The brown eye gene will mask, ie be dominant over the blue. Only if her bio mother carried the blue eyed gene, which would suggest she herself must have European ancestry, could she have a blue eyed child. Perhaps this’ll be a plot twist.
